Technical Leadership

Senior Robotics Technician and Technical Lead currently at 1X Technologies — owning critical robotic subsystems, driving full-system builds, and mentoring junior technicians. IPC-7711/7721 certified. A.S. in Electrical Engineering from American River College. A disciplined, hands-on engineer equally at home in a prototyping lab or an aviation repair bay.

Career History

Experience

2025 — Present
1X Technologies
Palo Alto, CA
Senior Robotics Engineering Technician — Technical Lead
  • Owned critical robotic subsystems — wiring harnesses, sensors, and electrical integrations — as primary technical point of contact across 10+ builds.
  • Led system-level diagnostics and root cause analysis across electrical, mechanical, and controls domains, resolving high-impact failures.
  • Coordinated full-system robot builds, aligning cross-functional engineering teams for integration readiness and successful deployment.
  • Drove time-critical demonstrations under tight deadlines, ensuring all subsystems met readiness requirements.
  • Mentored and trained 3–5+ junior technicians, elevating team efficiency and troubleshooting capability.
  • Analyzed diagnostic and fleet-level data to generate insights influencing system improvements and design decisions.
2025
Archer Aviation
Santa Clara, CA
EV Integration Technician
  • Built and owned complex wiring harness systems for eVTOL aircraft to aerospace standards using Molex connectors, crimp tools, and wire markers.
  • Integrated electrical, mechanical, and avionics systems across prototype and production builds.
  • Installed and configured NI PXI and SLSC automated test systems; performed signal integrity, continuity, and sensor/actuator validation.
  • Interpreted NX CAD engineering drawings for accurate structural integration and alignment.
2023 — 2024
TGH Aviation
Sacramento, CA
Electro-Mechanical Technician
  • Performed diagnostics, repair, and validation of aircraft electrical and mechanical components in compliance with FAA standards.
  • Utilized advanced test equipment to troubleshoot system failures and ensure operational readiness.
  • Maintained meticulous repair documentation improving traceability and regulatory compliance.
2019 — 2022
Prime Refrigeration
New York, NY
Service Technician
  • Diagnosed and repaired commercial HVAC systems, ensuring compliance with safety and performance standards.
  • Verified system alignment, torque, and operational efficiency during routine inspections.
Portfolio

Projects & Work

Aviation Repair
King Avionics Unit — Full Disassembly & Repair

Component-level teardown and repair of King Radio avionics navigation unit. Gear train inspection, PCB diagnostics, motor assembly validation, and reassembly to spec.

AvionicsPCB RepairFAAPrecision Assembly
PCB Diagnostics
King Radio PCB — Board-Level Diagnostics

Board-level inspection and fault isolation of King avionics PCB using instrumentation and trace analysis, cross-referenced against NAVAIR technical documentation.

PCBIPCNAVAIRDiagnostics
Technical Documentation
NAVAIR Technical Schematics

Working from U.S. Navy NAVAIR 05-40BH technical documentation for precision aviation instrument repair — cross-referencing component callouts for accurate fault diagnosis.

NAVAIRSchematicsAviation
Precision Repair
Aviation Servo Instrument — Full Rebuild

Full rebuild of a cylindrical aircraft servo instrument with integrated PCB electronics. Disassembled, diagnosed, repaired, and returned to operational readiness.

Servo SystemsAvionicsRebuild
Test & Calibration
Precision Test Bench Configuration

Multi-instrument test bench using decade resistance boxes, precision multimeters, and calibration equipment for aircraft instrument validation at TGH Aviation.

CalibrationInstrumentationAviation
IPC Certification
IPC-7711 / IPC-7721 Certification Training

Hands-on rework training across SMD and through-hole packages — QFP, PLCC, BGA, DIP, SOT, and 0603 — completed in pursuit of IPC certification (Credential ID: OVT-42/43).

IPC-7711IPC-7721SMD Rework
EE Coursework
Digital Logic Circuit — ARC Coursework

Complex digital logic circuit on breadboard featuring multiple ICs, LED display arrays, and a microcontroller, built during A.S. EE program at American River College.

Digital LogicMicrocontrollerARC
EE Lab Work
Signal Analysis Lab — Oscilloscope & DMM

Applied EE lab using Agilent DMM, Tektronix oscilloscope, and HP power supply to analyze circuit behavior and validate breadboard designs.

OscilloscopeSignal AnalysisEE Lab

Ferrari V8 Engine — 3D Printed Scale Model

Multi-part, multi-color 3D printed Ferrari V8 engine replica demonstrating mastery of FDM printing, multi-component design, and precision part assembly.

Reverse-engineered a German-manufactured ABS automotive component and replicated it in 3D print. Demonstrates measurement, modeling, and fabrication precision.

3D printed DINAN automotive badges across multiple iterations, progressively improving print quality, surface finish, and dimensional accuracy.
